sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for Power Pad games

special_mechanics.name: Power Pad Controller
special_mechanics.description: Floor mat game controller with twelve pressure-sensors (Side B) and eight buttons (Side A). Player physically runs, jumps, and steps on the pad to control gameplay, similar to modern dance pad games.

special_mechanics.name: Athletic Events Simulation
special_mechanics.description: Game simulates five Olympic-style challenges: Hurdles (timing-based), Hop A Log, Animal Trail, Rafting, and Dark Tunnel (dexterity-based). Movement control requires physical running and jumping motions on pad.

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Empirical/qualitative only. Source material (Power Pad Wikipedia article) documents game mechanics and control scheme but contains no specific physics parameters (velocities, accelerations, gravity values, or sub-pixel data). The game is controller-dependent rather than character-physics-focused, with gameplay centered on player's physical timing and coordination with step inputs on the pressure mat.
sources_used: Power Pad - Wikipedia (Athletic World and Running Stadium/Stadium Events/World Class Track Meet game descriptions)
